pr.title: Added fox item attr and some small fixes
pr.author: AaronB-Hub

summary: This PR refined item structs and item-var usage for Fox-related items and several common items. It added explicit attr structs for Fox blaster/laser/illusion and common items such as capsule, heart container, light-gun ray/beam, and fire flower, while renaming several anonymous xDD4-style runtime fields to semantic names like scale, angle, speed, pos, lifetime, and heal_amount. It also corrected code that had been accessing unrelated union members such as capsule for egg, fire flower, heart container, and Maxim Tomato item state.
what_changed: Added FoxBlasterAttr, FoxLaserAttr, and FoxIllusionAttr to src/melee/it/itCharItems.h.
Renamed FoxLaserVars runtime fields from xDD4/xDD8/xDDC/xDE0 to scale/angle/speed/pos with ip+DD4 offset comments.
Changed itfoxlaser.c to treat xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes as FoxLaserAttr instead of FoxLaserVars, using attr->lifetime and attr->scale.
Renamed the it_8029C504 parameter arg9 to speed in both itfoxlaser.c and itfoxlaser.h.
Split capsule special attributes from runtime vars by renaming ItCapsuleAttrs to ItCapsuleVars and adding ItCapsuleAttr for x4_specialAttributes.
Added HeartContainerAttr and updated itheart.c to read heal, velocity, flags, and rotation from the attr struct rather than from HeartContainerVars.
Reordered and clarified light-gun structs in itCommonItems.h, including ItLGunVars, ItLGunRayVars, ItLGunRayAttr, and ItLGunBeamAttr.
Renamed ItLGunBeamAttr.x0 to lifetime and updated itlgunbeam.c to use item_spec_attr->lifetime.
Added FFlowerAttr and updated itfflower.c to read special attributes as FFlowerAttr rather than FFlowerVars.
Corrected xDD4_itemVar union accesses: egg code now uses .egg, fire flower uses .fflower, heart container uses .HeartContainer, and Maxim Tomato uses .MaximTomato.heal_amount instead of .capsule.
Updated src/melee/it/types.h so the Item xDD4_itemVar union uses ItCapsuleVars capsule instead of the former ItCapsuleAttrs name.
smart_moves: Separated per-item runtime variables from article special attributes, reducing confusion between xDD4_itemVar state and xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes data.
Used offset comments such as ip+DD4, ip+DD8, and ip+DE0 to preserve layout reasoning while giving fields semantic names.
Preserved unknown fields as xN names where their meaning was not established, while naming only fields with clear usage such as lifetime, scale, angle, speed, pos, heal_amount, and max_scale.
Added observed data-value comments like [35], [3], [100], [1.5], and [0.0349066] to attr structs, making future data matching and validation easier.
Fixed misleading union member accesses that compiled through shared layout but obscured the actual item-specific state being used.
Kept the fox laser behavior stable while improving readability: velocity still derives from speed*cos(angle) and speed*sin(angle), and laser scale clamping still uses the article attr.
decomp_lessons: Do not reuse a convenient xDD4_itemVar union member name such as capsule for unrelated item types; add or use the item-specific union arm even when the memory layout overlaps.
Article special attributes and runtime item vars should be modeled as separate structs, usually with Attr for x4_specialAttributes and Vars for xDD4_itemVar storage.
When a field's behavior is clear from code, semantic names improve decomp maintainability without requiring every surrounding field to be understood.
Offset comments are useful during incremental struct recovery because they let renamed fields remain auditable against item-instance offsets.
Known constants from item data are valuable evidence for attr structs; record them as comments rather than over-naming fields with uncertain meanings.
Small signature cleanups, such as changing arg9 to speed, should be propagated to headers to keep call-site intent searchable.
naming_conventions: Use *Attr for structs pointed to by Article.x4_specialAttributes, e.g. FoxLaserAttr, ItCapsuleAttr, HeartContainerAttr, FFlowerAttr.
Use *Vars for runtime item variables stored under Item.xDD4_itemVar, e.g. FoxLaserVars, ItCapsuleVars, ItLGunVars, ItLGunRayVars.
Prefer semantic field names when usage is evident: scale, angle, speed, pos, lifetime, max_scale, heal_amount.
Keep unknown fields as offset-style names such as x8, xC, x10 when their meaning is not yet proven.
Use item-specific xDD4_itemVar union arms, such as egg, fflower, HeartContainer, and MaximTomato, instead of borrowing capsule.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renaming FoxLaserVars fields did not change layout: xDD4 became scale, xDD8 became angle, xDDC became speed, and xDE0 became pos.
Fox laser velocity calculations are expressed semantically as speed multiplied by cosf/sinf of angle, preserving the original math while clarifying variable roles.
Lifetime setup calls such as it_80275158 now read attr->lifetime for FoxLaserAttr and ItLGunBeamAttr instead of anonymous x0/xDD4 fields.
The PR uses attr structs with comments containing suspected item data values to support matching article-data layouts against DOL/data tables.
Union-member corrections are source-level clarity fixes that rely on the same xDD4_itemVar storage but make the intended item-specific layout explicit.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body notes that an earlier failed PR was closed so the author could resolve issues and open a new one; no exact failed checks or reviewer requests are included in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further name FoxBlasterAttr, FoxLaserAttr, or FoxIllusionAttr fields still left as x0/x4/x8.
Search for remaining xDD4_itemVar.capsule accesses in non-capsule item code.
Audit other item specialAttributes casts where *Vars may still be used instead of *Attr.
Check whether HeartContainerAttr x4 flags and x8/xC/x10 fields were later given semantic names.
Look for common-item attr structs with data-value comments that can be cross-referenced against item data tables.
